prediction.reasoning: Based on historical volatility patterns for industrial stocks, assuming approximately 20-25% annual volatility which translates to roughly 1.3-1.6% daily standard deviation. The point estimate assumes modest upward drift typical of equity markets over 21-day periods. Confidence intervals are constructed using normal distribution assumptions with increasing width for higher confidence levels, accounting for the inherent uncertainty in short-term price movements.
